name: nero-great-fire
description: A devastating fire burned for six days, destroying ten of Rome's fourteen districts under Emperor Nero's reign.

name: eruption-mount-vesuvius
description: The volcanic eruption buried the Roman cities of Pompeii and Herculaneum under meters of ash and pumice.

name: medieval-warm-period
description: A period of unusually warm climate in the North Atlantic enabled Viking exploration and agricultural expansion.

name: deforestation-mediterranean
description: Centuries of shipbuilding and agriculture stripped Mediterranean forests, causing widespread soil erosion.

name: land-reclamation-netherlands
description: The Dutch began systematically draining lakes and marshes using dikes and windmills to create farmland.

name: little-ice-age-onset
description: Global temperatures dropped, causing crop failures, famines, and the abandonment of Norse Greenland colonies.

name: irrigation-abandonment-southwest
description: Prolonged drought forced the Ancestral Puebloans to abandon their sophisticated irrigation systems and cliff dwellings.

name: european-famine-1315-1317
description: Torrential rains and crop failures across Northern Europe killed millions and ended a period of growth.

name: plague-rats-ships
description: Genoese trading ships carried plague-infected rats from Caffa to Sicily, introducing the Black Death to Europe.

name: plague-medieval-waves
friendly_name: Medieval Plague Waves Hit Europe Repeatedly
description: Recurring plague outbreaks after the initial Black Death continued to devastate European populations for decades.

name: horse-introduction-americas
description: Spanish conquistadors brought horses to the Americas, transforming Indigenous cultures and Great Plains ecology.

name: deforestation-britain
description: Britain's forests were nearly depleted by iron smelting demand, spurring the eventual shift to coal fuel.

name: rinderpest-africa-impact
description: A cattle plague killed up to 90% of herds in East Africa, devastating pastoral societies and enabling colonial conquest.

name: soil-exhaustion-mesopotamia
description: Irrigation salt buildup made Sumerian farmland unproductive, forcing civilizations to shift locations repeatedly.

name: nine-eleven-attacks
description: Terrorists hijacked four planes and attacked the World Trade Center and Pentagon, killing nearly 3,000 people.
